title: Year-aware covariate-shift weighting and expert blending
summary: The data span 2022-2025 with many drivers and likely rule/car changes, so the train-test mix is unlikely stationary.
rationale: The data span 2022-2025 with many drivers and likely rule/car changes, so the train-test mix is unlikely stationary. Domain-adaptation work suggests importance weighting or expert mixtures can help when the feature distribution shifts but the label mechanism is mostly stable.
implementation_hint: Train a discriminator to separate train rows from test rows using only features available at inference. Convert its OOF scores into instance weights for the main model, or blend a global model with year-specific experts using a gating feature such as predicted test-likeness. Also test leaving one year out to detect whether current CV is too optimistic about cross-year transfer.
expected_effect: Could improve public score if test is concentrated in a slightly shifted regime that the current pooled model underweights.
risk: Importance weights can become unstable and amplify noise; if shift is weak, this adds variance without benefit.
